name: Ixthra, Flesh Architect
oracle_text:
Menace
Whenever a nontoken creature dies, exile it. You may put a creature card exiled with ~ onto the battlefield under your control at the beginning of your next end step. If you do, it enters with a finality counter on it.
Creatures you control with finality counters on them get +2/+0 and have menace.
flavor_text: The House of Flesh does not kill. It repurposes.

validation_errors: Oracle text is 305 characters, exceeding the 300-char limit for this card type

design_notes: A mythic legendary tied to the House of Flesh lore — the eldritch horror mansion where cultists reshape living things. Mechanically complex and game-warping as a mythic should be. The death trigger exiles ALL nontoken creatures that die (yours and opponents'), giving you a delayed reanimation of one per death. The finality counter means reanimated creatures are one-use (they get exiled on death, per existing finality counter rules), preventing infinite loops. The +2/+0 and menace buff for finality-countered creatures creates an aggressive reanimator strategy — you're building a disposable army of repurposed dead. At 3/4 menace for 4 mana, the body is solid but not overwhelming; the power is in the engine. It's answerable by exile removal and graveyard hate (cards get exiled, but you can still remove finality-countered creatures). The delayed trigger (next end step, not immediately) gives opponents a window to respond.
